core_mechanism: Random-matrix-theory spectral filtering of a sample covariance/correlation matrix: eigen-decompose the matrix and compare its eigenvalue spectrum to the Marchenko-Pastur null distribution for pure noise, retaining only the eigenmodes whose eigenvalues lie outside the random-matrix bounds to reconstruct a denoised, signal-bearing operator.
brief_reason: The abstract applies spectral decomposition to split the empirical correlation matrix into a structured component (eigenvalues exceeding the Marchenko-Pastur bounds) and a random noise component, reconstructing the signal from only the outlier eigenmodes.
